title: ficus caulocarpa var. caulocarpa
intro: Ficus caulocarpa var. caulocarpa, also known as the Queensland bush fig or small-leaved fig, is a species of fig tree native to Australia and New Guinea. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. The leaves and roots of the plant can be used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
